title: Living in Vietnam on an Indian Passport: My Real Visa Setup
meta_title: Vietnam Visa for Indians: E-Visa & Visa Runs (2026)
subtitle: The e-visa, the port-of-entry rule, and how I actually handle visa runs — from someone living it in Da Nang
description: How Indians get a Vietnam visa and stay long-term: the official e-visa ($25 single / $50 multiple, up to 90 days), the port-of-entry rule, and real visa-run options — first-hand from an Indian living in Da Nang.

faqs.question: Do Indians need a visa for Vietnam?
faqs.answer: Yes. Indian passport holders need a visa. The simplest option is the official e-visa applied online at evisa.gov.vn — about $25 for single entry or $50 for multiple entry, valid for up to 90 days.

faqs.question: How much does the Vietnam e-visa cost for Indians?
faqs.answer: Paid directly to the government on the official site (evisa.gov.vn), it is roughly $25 for a single-entry e-visa and $50 for multiple entry. Third-party sites charge much more, so use the official portal.

faqs.question: How long can an Indian stay in Vietnam on an e-visa?
faqs.answer: Up to 90 days per e-visa. To stay longer, you do a visa run — leave the country and re-enter on a fresh e-visa.

faqs.question: Can I apply for a new Vietnam e-visa while still inside Vietnam?
faqs.answer: No. You can only apply for the next e-visa after you have exited Vietnam and have your exit stamp. Plan your visa run around that rule.

faqs.question: What is a Vietnam visa run?
faqs.answer: Leaving Vietnam — by a same-day land-border trip or a short flight to Bangkok, Kuala Lumpur or Singapore — and re-entering on a new e-visa. It is how long-stayers reset their 90 days.

faqs.question: Is the Vietnam e-visa hard to get for Indians?
faqs.answer: Not in most cases. On an Indian passport it is routine with clean documents. The main thing to get right is entering through the exact port of entry named on a single-entry e-visa.
